properties.remark: UDOT SENSOR I-80 AT MILE POST 78.
TREE BLOWN DOWN ON CAR IN DRIVEWAY.

A MAGNITUDE EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN WILLARD. THE TORNADO PATH WAS ONE MILE...STARTING AT 75 EAST 300 SOUTH...AND ENDING AT 300 EAST 230 SOUTH. THE DAMAGE FROM THE TORNADO INCLUDED SIDING...SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED...A COUPLE OF TREES SNAPPED...AND AN EMPTY SNOWMOBILE TRAILER TOSSED.
EF0 TORNADO OBSERVED AND PHOTOGRAPHED WEST OF MILFORD. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.
TWO HOMES DAMAGED BY DEBRIS FLOWS FROM THE CORNER CANYON BURN SCAR
SIX TO TWELVE INCHES OF WATER ACROSS CAPITOL GORGE SCENIC DRIVE. MINOR DAMAGE TO DIRT ROAD.
TREES DOWN NEAR 3800S AND REDWOOD ROAD.
FLASH FLOODING ALONG SULPHUR CREEK NEAR MILE MARKER 72 ON STATE ROUTE 24
FLASH FLOODING ALONG THE FREMONT RIVER NEAR THE EAST ENTRANCE TO THE PARK ALONG SR 24
WENDOVER POINT OF ENTRY ALONG I-80
SEMI-TRAILER BLOWN OVER AT MP 72 ON I-80
SEMI-TRAILER BLOWN OVER AT MP 85 ON I-80
GAGE ON THE PARIA RIVER AT US HIGHWAY 89. READING WENT FROM 3 CFS TO 4419 CFS IN 15 MINUTES. PEAK FLOW ESTIMATED AT 7328 CFS ONE HOUR LATER.
DEBRIS FROM A BURN AREA WAS WASHED INTO THE TOWN OF BIRDSEYE.
